---
title: "Statistical DUS Overexpression analysis"
author: "Oliwia Hillestad Waz"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
library(Biostrings)
library(readxl)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats) # Useful for reordering the species axis
library(scales) # Required for the rescale function

```

enrichment analysis performed based on DUS counts in each genome 
```{r}
#load genome sequences and clean them up 
seqs <- readDNAStringSet("C:/Users/oliwi/OneDrive - OsloMet/00. Master Thesies/Bioinformatics/Data/Chromo36_ecoli_pseudomallei.fasta") 

# Define the two genomes with contigs to target them for merging later on 
merge_targets <- c("Vogesella indigofera", "Gulbenkiania indica", "Burkholderia pseudomallei") 

# Create a grouping ID; this ID is ONLY used to decide which contigs belong togethe
grouping_id <- sapply(names(seqs), function(header) { 
  is_merge_target <- any(sapply(merge_targets, function(x) grepl(x, header))) 
  if (is_merge_target) { 
    header %>% str_remove("^\\S+\\s+") %>%  # Remove Accession (NZ_...) 
      str_remove(",.*$") %>%   # Remove everything after comma 
      str_remove("_\\d+$") %>% # Remove contig suffix (e.g., _101)
      str_remove("(?i)chromosome\\s*\\d+") %>% # Remove "chromosome 1" or "chromosome 2"
      str_trim() } else {   
        header } # keep other genomes as is 
  }) 

# Merge sequences 
split_seqs <- split(seqs, grouping_id) 
seqs <- DNAStringSet( lapply(split_seqs, 
                             function(x) do.call(
                               xscat, as.list(x)))) 

# Final Cleaning of the names - remove the keys (which still have the Genus)
names(seqs) <- names(split_seqs) %>%
  str_remove("^[A-Z]{1,3}_[A-Z0-9]+\\.\\d+\\s+") %>%  
  str_remove("(?i)\\s+chromosome.*$") %>% 
  str_remove("(?i)\\s+complete genome.*$") %>% 
  str_remove("(?i)\\s+whole genome shotgun.*$") %>% 
  str_remove(",.*$") %>%   
  ifelse(grepl("^Vogesella", .), 
         str_remove(., "\\s+Ga\\d+$"), .) %>%   # Remove Ga0215675 ONLY from Vogesella
  ifelse(grepl("^Gulbenkiania", .), 
         str_remove(., "\\s+DSM_\\d+$"), .) %>% # Remove DSM_17901 ONLY from Gulbenkiania
  str_trim()

# Verification 
print(names(seqs))


```

obs and genome properties 
```{r}
# load DUS counts 
obs_df <- read_excel("C:/Users/oliwi/OneDrive - OsloMet/00. Master Thesies/Bioinformatics/Strain_list.xlsx")

head(obs_df)

# Compute genome properties
genome_props <- data.frame(
  Genome = names(seqs),
  Length = width(seqs),
  GC = letterFrequency(seqs, c("G","C"), as.prob = TRUE) %>% rowSums()
)

# Merge observed counts with genome properties
df <- obs_df %>%
  inner_join(genome_props, by = "Genome")


```

compute expected counts (GC-based)
```{r}
DUS_list <- list(
  DUS10 = c("GTTGGCCGMA"),
  DUS9  = c("GTTGGCCGM"),
  DUS8  = c("GTTGGCCG"),
  DUS7  = c("TTGGCCG"),
  DUS6  = c("TGGCCG"),
  DUS5  = c("GGCCG"),
  DUS4  = c("GCCG")
)

# 1. Function to calculate probability of a motif given GC content
calc_motif_prob <- function(motif, gc) {
  n_gc <- str_count(motif, "[GC]")
  n_at <- str_count(motif, "[AT]")
  n_m  <- str_count(motif, "M")
  # Probabilities
  p_gc <- gc / 2
  p_at <- (1 - gc) / 2
  p_m  <- 0.5  # Because (gc/2) + ((1-gc)/2) = 0.5
  # Total probability
  return((p_gc^n_gc) * (p_at^n_at) * (p_m^n_m))
}

# 2. Loop through the DUS_list to create Expected and Enrichment columns
for (dus_name in names(DUS_list)) {
  motif <- DUS_list[[dus_name]]
  k <- nchar(motif)
  # Calculate Expected
  exp_col_name <- paste0(dus_name, "_exp")
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(!!exp_col_name := 2 * Length * calc_motif_prob(motif, GC))
  # Calculate Enrichment Ratio (Observed / Expected)
  # Assumes your obs_df has columns named exactly "DUS10", "DUS9", etc.
  enrich_col_name <- paste0(dus_name, "_enrichment")
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(!!enrich_col_name := .data[[dus_name]] / .data[[exp_col_name]])
}

# Check the results
head(df %>% select(Genome, ends_with("enrichment")))

```


heatmap 
```{r}

# 1. Reshape the data for plotting
# This takes the enrichment columns and stacks them
df_long <- df %>%
  select(Genome, ends_with("_enrichment")) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = ends_with("_enrichment"),
    names_to = "Motif",
    values_to = "Enrichment_Ratio"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Motif = str_replace(Motif, "_enrichment", " ratio")) %>%
  # Use the double colon to force dplyr
  dplyr::rename(Species = Genome)


# 2. Order the Motifs from 10 to 4
df_long$Motif <- factor(df_long$Motif, 
                        levels = c("DUS10 ratio", "DUS9 ratio", "DUS8 ratio", 
                                   "DUS7 ratio", "DUS6 ratio", "DUS5 ratio", "DUS4 ratio"))

# 3. Construct the Heatmap
ggplot(df_long, 
       aes(x = Motif, 
           y = fct_reorder(Species, Enrichment_Ratio, .fun = max), 
           fill = Enrichment_Ratio)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") + 
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Enrichment_Ratio, 1)), size = 3, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("#f7fcf5", "#f7fcf5", "#FFCC66", "#CC9933", "#CC9900"), # Adjusted for better contrast
    trans = "log10",
    name = "Enrichment\nRatio (log10)",
    breaks = c(1, 10, 100, 1000, 10000),
    labels = scales::comma
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "ChromoDUS Enrichment Across Species",
    subtitle = "Values represent Observed/Expected ratios based on GC-bias",
    x = "Motif Length",
    y = "Bacterial Species"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

```


Calculate P-values and Significance
```{r}
df_long <- df %>%
  select(Genome, ends_with("_enrichment"), ends_with("_exp"), 
         DUS10, DUS9, DUS8, DUS7, DUS6, DUS5, DUS4) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(DUS10, DUS9, DUS8, DUS7, DUS6, DUS5, DUS4),
    names_to = "Motif_Name",
    values_to = "Observed"
  ) %>%
  # Match the expected values to the observed counts
  mutate(
    Expected = case_when(
      Motif_Name == "DUS10" ~ DUS10_exp,
      Motif_Name == "DUS9"  ~ DUS9_exp,
      Motif_Name == "DUS8"  ~ DUS8_exp,
      Motif_Name == "DUS7"  ~ DUS7_exp,
      Motif_Name == "DUS6"  ~ DUS6_exp,
      Motif_Name == "DUS5"  ~ DUS5_exp,
      Motif_Name == "DUS4"  ~ DUS4_exp
    ),
    Enrichment_Ratio = Observed / Expected,
    # Calculate Poisson P-value (lower.tail = FALSE gives P(X > x))
    P_val = ppois(Observed - 1, lambda = Expected, lower.tail = FALSE)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Motif_Name) %>%
  mutate(P_adj = p.adjust(P_val, method = "BH")) %>% # FDR correction
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Signif_Label = case_when(
    P_adj > 0.05  ~ "*",
    TRUE ~ ""
  )) %>%
  mutate(Motif = paste0(Motif_Name, " ratio")) %>%
  dplyr::rename(Species = Genome)

#to check which have insignificant p-values 
ggplot(df_long, 
       aes(x = factor(Motif, levels = c("DUS10 ratio", "DUS9 ratio", "DUS8 ratio", 
                                       "DUS7 ratio", "DUS6 ratio", "DUS5 ratio", "DUS4 ratio")), 
           y = fct_reorder(Species, Enrichment_Ratio, .fun = max), 
           fill = Enrichment_Ratio)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") + 
  # Add the Significance stars
  geom_text(aes(label = Signif_Label), color = "black", vjust = 0.8, size = 5) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("#f7fcf5", "#e5f5e0", "#CC9933", "#CC9900", "#663300"),
    trans = "log10",
    name = "Enrichment\nRatio (log10)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Significant DUS Enrichment Across Species",
    subtitle = "Asterisks indicate insignificant FDR-adjusted P-values (* > 0.05)",
    x = "Motif",
    y = "Species"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))



```
